text: Half-marathon runners have similar anthropometric and physiological characteristics to full-marathon runners due to the affinity of metabolic demands between the two race distances.
Half-marathon performance is positively associated with superior aerobic capacity, including maximal oxygen uptake, anaerobic threshold, and running economy.

Key training characteristics associated with better half-marathon performance include greater sport experience, higher weekly distance, faster training speed, higher frequency of sessions, and longer single endurance run distance.
Lower adiposity-related measures (body mass, body mass index, body fat percentage, and skinfold thickness) are associated with better half-marathon performance.
The majority of half-marathon runners are amateur runners who would benefit from sex-, age-, and performance-tailored exercise prescription.
